top_signals.title: Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh's testimony to Senate banking committee hits on economy, interest rates
top_signals.summary: As part of congressionally mandated Capitol Hill appearances for the central bank leader, Warsh spoke Tuesday to the House financial services committee.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Bank of England appoints Rhys Phillips as the new Chief Cashier
top_signals.summary: The Governor of the Bank of England, Andrew Bailey, has announced that Rhys Phillips will be the next Chief Cashier and Director of Notes. He will take up the role on 19 October 2026.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Conflicts of interest are back — and more blatant than ever
top_signals.summary: Democrats would do well to focus their fire on the use of public office by Donald Trump’s circle for private gain
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: U.S. commercial crude oil inventories have decreased in June
top_signals.summary: For the week ending June 19, 2026, U.S. refineries processed 17.1 million barrels per day (b/d) of crude oil, down 81,000 b/d from the previous week, and they operated at 96.1% capacity utilization. Gasoline production averaged 9.5 million b/d, and distillate production increased to 5.2 million b/d.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Energy IPOs surge as investors hunt for ways to play AI boom
top_signals.summary: Companies coming to market raise money at fastest pace this century, although many stocks perform poorly later
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: As the AI rally loses steam, how resilient are Japanese stocks?
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: SpaceX stock sinks below $135 IPO price for the first time
top_signals.summary: SpaceX shares fell for a fourth-straight session on Wednesday days after entering the Nasdaq-100
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.summary: Given at the Natixis CIB Private Debt Forum

top_signals.title: The Magyar revolution: Hungary’s new era
top_signals.summary: The prime minister has begun dismantling the old Orbán machine. But is the country just exchanging one strongman for another?

top_signals.summary: One simple trick to share it equally

top_signals.title: Messi punishes England to drive Argentina into World Cup final
top_signals.summary: Reigning men’s champions celebrate comeback 2-1 win after opponents retreat into defensive shell

top_signals.title: U.S. refining capacity decreased during 2025
top_signals.summary: U.S. operable atmospheric distillation capacity, the primary measure of refinery capacity, totaled 18.2 million barrels per calendar day (b/cd) on January 1, 2026-down over 250,000 b/cd (about 1%) compared with January 1, 2025-according to our latest annual Refinery Capacity Report.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

alerts.title: Brewdog owner shrugs off James Watt takeover bid
alerts.summary: The owner of Brewdog has insisted the company is “not for sale” after co-founder James Watt launched a dramatic takeover bid for the craft beer firm.  Irwin Simon, boss of Brewdog’s US owner, Tilray, told City AM he intends to retain the pub chain and brewery just hours after Watt said he had submitted a [...]
